title: Authors and Citation
text: Cota M (2026). twostraindengue: Calibration Tools Two-Strain Dengue Transmission Model. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://sauldiazinfante.github.io/two-strain-dengue-calibration/, https://github.com/SaulDiazInfante/two-strain-dengue-calibration.
code: @Manual{,   title = {twostraindengue: Calibration Tools for a Two-Strain Dengue Transmission Model},   author = {Manuel Cota},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000,     https://sauldiazinfante.github.io/two-strain-dengue-calibration/},   url = {https://github.com/SaulDiazInfante/two-strain-dengue-calibration}, }

title: twostraindengue
text: twostraindengue research-focused R package simulating calibrating two-strain dengue transmission model. repository includes reusable model code, raw cleaned data assets, reproducible analysis scripts, tests, pkgdown-ready documentation layout.

title: twostraindengue
code: library(twostraindengue)  parameters <- default_parameters() initial_state <- default_state(parameters) time_grid <- seq(0, 6, by = 0.1)  model_output <- run_model(initial_state, time_grid, parameters) reported_cases <- extract_reported_cases(model_output)  study_window <- load_study_data() analysis <- run_hermosillo_2010_analysis(   config = list(outputs = list(write_outputs = FALSE, show_progress = FALSE)) )

title: twostraindengue
text: R/: package functions data/: packaged example datasets inst/extdata/raw/: raw study inputs inst/scripts/: reproducible analysis scripts outputs/: generated figures calibration tables vignettes/: long-form analysis package documentation tests/testthat/: automated tests

Calibration Example Inputs — calibration_example
text: named list 3 entries: parameters Named numeric vector returned default_parameters(). initial_state Named numeric vector returned default_state(). observation_window Integer vector defining short calibration window.

title: Calibration Objective Wrappers — calibration_objectives
text: functions provide package-facing wrappers original parameterisation reparameterisations based R01, R02, R0.

title: Calibration Objective Wrappers — calibration_objectives
code: negative_loglikelihood_original_scale(   par,   observation_times,   observed_cases,   initial_state,   time_grid,   fixed_parameters )  negative_loglikelihood_r0(   par,   observation_times,   observed_cases,   initial_state,   time_grid,   fixed_parameters )  negative_loglikelihood_r01(   par,   observation_times,   observed_cases,   initial_state,   time_grid,   fixed_parameters )  negative_loglikelihood_r02(   par,   observation_times,   observed_cases,   initial_state,   time_grid,   fixed_parameters )

title: Calibration Objective Wrappers — calibration_objectives
text: par Numeric vector betaH first position wrapper-specific second parameter second position: betaM negative_loglikelihood_original_scale(), R01 negative_loglikelihood_r01(), R02 negative_loglikelihood_r02(), R0 negative_loglikelihood_r0(). observation_times Observation times simulation grid. observed_cases Two-column matrix observed classical hemorrhagic case counts. initial_state Named numeric state vector used run_model(). time_grid Numeric vector solver times. fixed_parameters Named numeric vector containing fixed model parameters.

title: Calibration Objective Wrappers — calibration_objectives
text: scalar negative Poisson log-likelihood. scalar negative Poisson log-likelihood. scalar negative Poisson log-likelihood. scalar negative Poisson log-likelihood.

title: Calibration Utilities — calibration_tools
text: helpers support likelihood evaluation, reparameterisation reproduction numbers, likelihood surfaces, profile-likelihood summaries.

title: Calibration Utilities — calibration_tools
code: evaluate_negative_loglikelihood(   beta_h,   beta_m,   observation_times,   observed_cases,   initial_state,   time_grid,   fixed_parameters )  compute_reproduction_components(initial_state, fixed_parameters)  beta_m_from_reproduction(   beta_h,   reproduction_value,   mode,   initial_state,   fixed_parameters )  evaluate_reparameterised_loglikelihood(   beta_h,   reproduction_value,   mode,   observation_times,   observed_cases,   initial_state,   time_grid,   fixed_parameters )  evaluate_loglikelihood_grid(x_values, y_values, evaluator)  relative_likelihood(loglikelihood)  profile_interval(values, relative_profile, cutoff = 0.146)

title: Calibration Utilities — calibration_tools
text: beta_h Human--mosquito transmission coefficient. beta_m Mosquito--human transmission coefficient. observation_times Observation times simulation grid. observed_cases Two-column matrix observed classical hemorrhagic case counts. initial_state Named numeric state vector used run_model(). time_grid Numeric vector solver times. fixed_parameters Named numeric vector containing fixed model parameters. reproduction_value Value R01, R02, R0, depending mode. mode Character scalar selecting "R01", "R02", "R0". x_values Numeric vector first parameter axis. y_values Numeric vector second parameter axis. evaluator Function taking two-element numeric vector returning negative log-likelihood. loglikelihood Numeric vector matrix log-likelihood values. values Numeric vector defining profile grid. relative_profile Numeric vector relative profile likelihood values. cutoff Relative-likelihood cutoff used define interval.

title: Calibration Utilities — calibration_tools
text: scalar negative Poisson log-likelihood. named numeric vector reproduction-number components C1, C2, C3. implied mosquito--human transmission coefficient betaM. scalar negative Poisson log-likelihood converting reproduction-number parameterisation back betaM. matrix log-likelihood values supplied grid. Relative likelihood values normalised maximum entry. named numeric vector entries lower upper.

what: Model Defaults and Simulation Helpers — model_setup
title: Model Defaults and Simulation Helpers — model_setup
text: functions expose default parameterisation, initial conditions, raw Hermosillo study data loader, ODE simulation workflow used throughout package.

title: Model Defaults and Simulation Helpers — model_setup
code: default_parameters()  default_state(parameters = default_parameters())  load_study_data(   path = system.file("extdata", "raw", "study_data_dengue_hemorrhagic_fever.csv", package     = "twostraindengue"),   rows = 35:50 )  run_model(   initial_state,   time_grid,   parameters,   ode_function = dengue_model_ode )  extract_reported_cases(model_output)

title: Model Defaults and Simulation Helpers — model_setup
text: parameters Named numeric parameter vector. path Path raw study CSV. default, resolves file shipped inst/extdata/raw/. rows Integer row indices select raw study data. initial_state Named numeric state vector passed deSolve::ode(). time_grid Numeric vector solver times. ode_function ODE callback used deSolve::ode(). Defaults package's internal two-strain dengue system. model_output Matrix data frame returned run_model().

title: Model Defaults and Simulation Helpers — model_setup
text: named numeric vector containing default model parameters. named numeric vector containing default initial state ODE system, including cumulative reported-case state z. data frame columns week, classical, hemorrhagic. matrix ODE output returned deSolve::ode(). two-column matrix classical hemorrhagic reported-case trajectories extracted ODE output.

title: Model Defaults and Simulation Helpers — model_setup
code: parameters <- default_parameters() state <- default_state(parameters) time_grid <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)  model_output <- run_model(state, time_grid, parameters) head(extract_reported_cases(model_output)) #>      classical hemorrhagic #> [1,]  4.000000   1.0000000 #> [2,]  4.574777   0.9295072 #> [3,]  5.154836   0.8702416 #> [4,]  5.747903   0.8217707 #> [5,]  6.360186   0.7837030 #> [6,]  6.996752   0.7556928

title: Plot a Relative Likelihood Surface — plot_likelihood_surface
text: x_values Numeric vector x-axis. y_values Numeric vector y-axis. surface Numeric matrix likelihood values. x_label X-axis label. y_label Y-axis label. point Optional numeric vector length 2 highlight plot. label Optional legend label point. main Plot title.

title: Plot a Relative Likelihood Surface — plot_likelihood_surface

what: Plot a Model Fit Against Observed Cases — plot_model_fit
title: Plot a Model Fit Against Observed Cases — plot_model_fit

title: Plot a Model Fit Against Observed Cases — plot_model_fit
code: plot_model_fit(   model_output,   data,   rows = seq_len(nrow(data)),   title = "Model Fit",   week_origin = min(data$week[rows]),   xlim = NULL,   ylim = NULL )

title: Plot a Model Fit Against Observed Cases — plot_model_fit
text: model_output Matrix data frame returned run_model(). data data frame columns week, classical, hemorrhagic. rows Integer row indices display. title Plot title. week_origin Week number corresponding simulation time zero. xlim Optional x-axis limits. ylim Optional y-axis limits.

title: Plot a Model Fit Against Observed Cases — plot_model_fit
text: Invisibly returns fitted case matrix used overlay.

text: data data frame columns week, classical, hemorrhagic. rows Integer row indices display. title Plot title. ylim Optional y-axis limits.

what: Plot a Relative Profile Likelihood — plot_profile_likelihood
title: Plot a Relative Profile Likelihood — plot_profile_likelihood
text: Plots one-dimensional relative profile likelihood curve optionally annotates corresponding confidence interval.

title: Plot a Relative Profile Likelihood — plot_profile_likelihood
code: plot_profile_likelihood(   values,   relative_profile,   x_label,   interval = c(lower = NA_real_, upper = NA_real_),   interval_label = NULL,   main = "Relative Profile Likelihood" )

title: Plot a Relative Profile Likelihood — plot_profile_likelihood
text: values Numeric vector defining profile grid. relative_profile Numeric vector relative profile likelihood values. x_label X-axis label. interval Named numeric vector lower upper. interval_label Optional character vector interval legend. main Plot title.

title: Plot a Relative Profile Likelihood — plot_profile_likelihood
text: Invisibly returns relative_profile.

what: Run the Hermosillo 2010 Analysis Workflow — run_hermosillo_2010_analysis
title: Run the Hermosillo 2010 Analysis Workflow — run_hermosillo_2010_analysis
text: Runs full calibration visualization workflow Hermosillo 2010 dengue case study using compact nested configuration. workflow reads defaults packaged JSON config, supports JSON override files, also accepts -memory config overrides quick experiments.

title: Run the Hermosillo 2010 Analysis Workflow — run_hermosillo_2010_analysis
code: run_hermosillo_2010_analysis(   config = NULL,   config_path = NULL,   data = NULL,   ... )

title: Run the Hermosillo 2010 Analysis Workflow — run_hermosillo_2010_analysis
text: config Optional named list nested sections data, observed_rows, grids, outputs, optimisation. config_path Optional path JSON file containing configuration overrides merged top packaged defaults. data Optional study data overriding data source specified config. ... Optional legacy flat arguments previous interface. converted config overrides backwards compatibility.

title: Run the Hermosillo 2010 Analysis Workflow — run_hermosillo_2010_analysis
text: named list containing normalized config, fitted parameters, likelihood surfaces, profile summaries, reproduction-number estimates, summary data frames, generated output file paths.

title: Run the Hermosillo 2010 Analysis Workflow — run_hermosillo_2010_analysis
code: analysis <- run_hermosillo_2010_analysis(   config = list(     observed_rows = 1:3,     grids = list(       time = list(         full = seq(0, 5, by = 0.5),         calibration = seq(0, 2, by = 0.5),         fit = seq(0, 5, by = 0.5)       ),       parameters = list(         beta_h = seq(1, 1.4, length.out = 3),         beta_m = seq(0.001, 0.003, length.out = 3),         r01 = seq(1.7, 1.9, length.out = 3),         r02 = seq(0.003, 0.0034, length.out = 3),         r0 = seq(1.3, 1.4, length.out = 3)       )     ),     outputs = list(write_outputs = FALSE, show_progress = FALSE)   ) )  analysis$reproduction_numbers #>         R01         R02          R0  #> 0.614394327 0.001167333 0.784577377

title: twostraindengue 0.0.0.9000
text: Bootstrapped package-oriented repository structure. Moved analysis outputs timestamped outputs/ subdirectories. Added initial test scaffolding CI/CD placeholders. Switched package documentation maintenance roxygen2-generated man/ NAMESPACE outputs. Expanded CI workflows multi-platform R CMD check, coverage reporting, pkgdown site builds. Added lightweight examples, vignettes, tests packaged Hermosillo 2010 workflow.
